title: Weekly Surge In Google Trends For Emergency Hvac Repair Signals Consumer Stress Lifting Xly
thesis: A sudden weekly spike in emergency HVAC repair searches indicates rising consumer stress and likely increased discretionary spending on urgent home services. Consumer urgency on home repairs often drives discretionary sector strength.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly HVAC emergency repair search volume
signal_logic: Enter long XLY when weekly HVAC emergency repair search volume rises 15% over prior 3-week average
entry_exit: Entry: Enter long XLY when weekly HVAC emergency repair search volume rises 15% over prior 3-week average Exit: Exit after 4 weeks or when search volume falls below prior 3-week average
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly HVAC emergency repair search volume via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Weekly search volume for emergency HVAC repair fluctuates seasonally and on weather-related stress, causing multiple spikes annually.

market_impact.rationale: Consumer urgency on home repairs often drives discretionary sector strength.

trade_logic.entry: Enter long XLY when weekly HVAC emergency repair search volume rises 15% over prior 3-week average
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 4 weeks or when search volume falls below prior 3-week average

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Weekly search volume for emergency HVAC repair fluctuates seasonally and on weather-related stress, causing multiple spikes annually.

title: Daily Spike In Freight Rail Carloads Signals Industrial Growth Pressure
thesis: A sharp daily increase in freight rail carloads indicates rising industrial activity and supply chain demand pressure. Industrial sector benefits directly from freight volume expansions.
data_sources: Federal Railroad Administration daily freight carloads
signal_logic: Enter long XLI if daily rail carloads increase 5% over 5-day moving average
entry_exit: Entry: Enter long XLI if daily rail carloads increase 5% over 5-day moving average Exit: Exit after 7 trading days or if carloads revert below 5-day average
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Federal Railroad Administration daily freight carloads via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Freight volumes vary frequently with industrial activity and often show bursts around economic data releases.
